appName: Brand name. Do not translate — keep as 'Pluralscape' in all locales.
ok: Generic affirmation button label. Use the target language's conventional short affirmation ('OK', 'Aceptar', etc.). Title case where locale convention applies.
cancel: Button label for aborting the current action (modal, form, operation). Not 'delete' — the user is stepping back, not destroying anything.
save: Button label for persisting changes. Used on forms and editors. Should feel like a commit action, not a back-up action.
back: Navigation label for returning to the previous screen. Very short; ideally 1–2 words.
loading: Indeterminate progress label shown while data is fetched. Can include an ellipsis or animated dots in the translation if the language convention allows.
error: Generic error label shown when a specific message isn't available. Neutral tone, not alarming.
